name: Debts — clear a debt & add a new one
summary: The journey a debt takes from the list, through a full clear (cash debits + a Debt-repayment txn logs), to gone. The pay-down rungs are healthy; the missing rung is what happens to a debt that's whittled to ~$0 — there's no clean archive, and the only exit (Delete) uses a native confirm.

steps.title: Open Debts
steps.does: You land on the Immediate Debts list (it lives on the Dashboard; the Analysis 'Debts →' link routes here too).
steps.plain: Opening the Debts collection. The list is one of the cards on the home screen — there is no separate Debts page, the 'Debts →' link just scrolls you to the same card.

steps.title: List renders (priority / strategy order)
steps.does: Active debts (paid + viaRent excluded from the headline total) render as tiles, sorted by priority; an IMMEDIATE total shows at the top.
steps.plain: A GET that returns every unpaid debt row and lays them out. The headline 'total' is the sum of the manual-pay rows. Avalanche/snowball ordering itself lives in getTopPriorityDebt (avalanche = highest rate first, snowball = smallest balance first).

steps.title: Headline total can disagree with the Payday canvas
steps.does: The Debts-card headline sums ALL non-viaRent unpaid debts; the Payday canvas reads only debts with a due-date inside this cycle. Same concept, two readers — they drift.
steps.wired: card total: renderDebtTiles imm-total (sum of all !viaRent) vs canvas: getActiveDebtsDueBeforePayday (delayDate within cycle only)
steps.plain: FR-07. Two different Postman endpoints answer 'what do I owe?' off the same debts table but with different WHERE clauses — the card counts every manual debt, the canvas counts only ones due before payday. They return different numbers and no one reconciles them. Violates INV-11 / INV-18.

steps.title: Tap a debt → modal opens
steps.does: Tapping a tile opens the debt detail modal with name, amount, rate, due date, notes, and a payoff-ETA line.
steps.plain: Opening the detail view for one debt row so you can act on it.

steps.title: Tap "Mark Cleared"
steps.does: You declare this debt paid off in full.
steps.plain: Fires the 'clear this debt' request. Note there is NO partial pay-down anywhere — the only pay action is a full clear (or hand-editing the amount). One concept: clear-in-full.

steps.title: markPaid composes cash + flag atomically
steps.does: Records a 'Debt repayment' outflow txn for the full amount AND flips the debt to paid:true, inside one writer. Aborts (and leaves the debt un-cleared) if the txn fails.
steps.plain: A composite endpoint: in one call it writes the cash-out transaction, debits the balance, and stamps the debt 'paid' with a back-reference to that txn (so it can be cleanly undone). CREATE-strict — if the txn write fails the whole thing rolls back, so you never get a 'paid' debt with no matching money movement.

steps.title: Cash debited + Debt-repayment txn logged
steps.does: Balance drops by the debt amount; a Debt repayment transaction appears in history.
steps.plain: The money side. Walk: bal 4800 → 4583 after clearing a $217 debt, one 'Debt repayment $217' txn in the ledger. This is the healthy rung — the cash and the record land together.

steps.title: Debt drops off the list (paid:true)
steps.does: renderDebtTiles re-runs; the cleared debt is filtered out (filter(!paid)); a celebration overlay + haptic fire; if the list is now empty the 'No Immediate Debts' congrats shows.
steps.plain: After clearing, the GET no longer returns that row (the tile filter hides paid:true), so it simply vanishes from the grid. The row still exists in the table with paid:true — it's hidden, not deleted.

steps.title: A whittled-down ($0-ish) debt has no archive affordance
steps.does: SHOULD exist here: if you edit a debt's amount down to ~$0 over time (instead of one full Mark-Cleared), there's no 'this is done — archive it' button. It just sits at a near-zero amount in the active list.
steps.wired: (missing — no archive/clear-at-zero affordance; saveDebt only edits amt via BRAIN.debts.update, never auto-clears at 0)
steps.plain: The MISSING rung. Editing the amount to 0 is a valid path (the amount field is freely editable) but nothing detects 'this debt is effectively gone' and offers to file it away. A $0 row lingers in the active list with no clean exit — the only way out is Delete (next step), which is the wrong tool because it implies 'never happened' rather than 'paid off, keep the history'.

steps.title: Delete uses a native confirm()
steps.does: The only way to remove a lingering/cleared debt is Delete, which pops a browser-native confirm() dialog before routing to BRAIN.debts.delete.
steps.plain: CLAUDE.md §8 violation: financial flows must use the in-app EDIT_MODAL.openConfirm, not the OS-native confirm() box. (saveNewDebt's linked-liability prompt at 8285 is a second native confirm.) Delete also throws away the debt's history — wrong instrument for a paid-off debt, which is why the missing archive rung at step 9 matters.

steps.title: Add a debt → it appears
steps.does: From the '+ Add Debt' button you fill name/amount/rate/due/notes/type, save, and the new tile shows in the list.
steps.plain: A POST that inserts a new debt row (with an ID-collision guard so two debts never share an id) and re-renders the list so the tile appears. Adding a debt does NOT move any cash — it just records the obligation. The 'is this linked to your car/credit card?' prompt mid-save is a native confirm() (§8 caveat, line 8285).
